title: Cloudflare Zone HTTP 5xx and Origin Error Rate
dataSources: Cloudflare Logpush `http_requests` dataset ingested with app-recommended sourcetype (commonly `cloudflare:json` per HEC job configuration)
spl:
index=web sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-1h
| eval edge_status=tonumber(EdgeResponseStatus)
| eval origin_status=tonumber(OriginResponseStatus)
| where edge_status>=500 OR origin_status>=500
| stats count by ClientRequestHost, EdgeResponseStatus, OriginResponseStatus, RayID
| sort -count
| head 50
description: Elevated 5xx responses at the edge or from the origin indicate failing pools, overloaded applications, or deliberate application-layer attacks. Ray ID–level aggregation supports fast hand-off between CDN and application owners.
value: Protects revenue and SLA-backed APIs by detecting origin brownouts minutes before synthetic monitoring catches them on low-traffic paths.
implementation: Create a Logpush job for `http_requests` to Splunk HEC; set sourcetype to `cloudflare:json` if following Cloudflare’s Splunk integration guide, or the dataset-specific sourcetype from the app’s Details section. Include `EdgeResponseStatus`, `OriginResponseStatus`, `ClientRequestHost`, and `RayID` in field selection. Alert when 5xx count exceeds a rolling baseline per hostname.
visualization: Time chart (5xx rate), Table (hostname, edge vs origin status), Single value (SLA breach indicator).

references.title: Cloudflare Docs — Enable Logpush to Splunk
